facts.context: If a test has a standard error of measurement of 15 points, itis correct to conclude that
facts.truth: about 68% of the individuals with a given cure score will have observed scores within 15 points of the true score
distractors: about 68% of the observed scores for the test population lie within 15 points of the mean score
about 15% of the scores are in error (i.e., they are not measuring the trait reliably)
differences of up to 30 points represent the same true score

facts.context: A researcher interested in examining the potential impact of parent alcoholism on child and family development recruits 12-year-olds (n = 100), 13-year-olds (n = 100), and 14-year-olds (n = 100)—half of whom have an alcoholic parent and half of whom do not—into a multiple-year longitudinal study assessing various outcomes. This study is best characterized as:
facts.truth: A cross-sequential cohort design
distractors: A true experiment
A natural experiment
A cross-sectional cohort design

facts.context: Statistical regression is a potential threat to a study’s internal validity when:
facts.truth: participants are selected for inclusion in the study because of their extremely high or low scores on the pretest.
distractors: there is a statistically different number of participants in the study’s treatment groups.
there is a statistically significant difference in the number of participants who drop out of the different treatment groups during the course of the study.
the researcher is unable to randomly assign participants to the different treatment groups.

facts.context: Three types of prevention have been identified in community mental health. Secondary prevention attempts to
facts.truth: reduce the severity of problems
distractors: deal with problems before they occur
prevent relapses of problems
prevent community disintegration

facts.context: You receive a call from Mrs. Wang who is very upset because her 14-year-old daughter witnessed the assault of her best friend three days ago and is very distraught. The girl hasn’t slept, won’t eat, and can’t stop crying. You have limited experience working with adolescents and in providing crisis intervention services. However, there is no one else in the community who is more experienced than you are. As an ethical psychologist, you will:
facts.truth: see Mrs. Wang’s daughter in therapy only until the crisis has ended or until you locate alternative services.
distractors: agree to see Mrs. Wang’s daughter in therapy since you’ve had some experience providing crisis intervention experiences.
inform Mrs. Wang about your lack of experience and let her decide if she wants you to provide therapy to her daughter.
inform Mrs. Wang that you cannot see her daughter because of your lack of experience.

facts.context: Alice A., who has a master's degree in clinical psychology but is not licensed, is hired by a community mental health clinic to, among other things, administer several standard psychological tests to adult clients. According to ethical guidelines, this is:
facts.truth: acceptable as long as a professional clinical psychologist supervises Alice’s activities.
distractors: acceptable as long as a professional clinical psychologist co-signs Alice’s evaluations.
unacceptable since Alice is not a licensed psychologist.
unacceptable since Alice does not have a doctoral degree.

facts.context: The primary function of the psychology licensing board is best described as:
facts.truth: protecting the public welfare.
distractors: developing laws that govern the practice of psychology.
providing sanctions for unethical and illegal behavior on the part of psychologists.
accrediting graduate programs in psychology.

facts.context: A psychologist is appointed by the Court to examine a defendant. The psychologist should
facts.truth: begin the evaluation by informing the defendant that anything that is discussed maybe talked about in open court
distractors: begin the evaluation by asking the defendant to indicate information that should be kept confidential
refuse to do the evaluation unless the Court agrees thar it will be kept confidential
discuss confidentiality only if the defendant asks about it

facts.context: According to HERSEY and BLANCHARD’S SITUATIONAL LEADERSHIP MODEL, leader style is described in terms of task and relationship orientation and optimal style depends on job maturity and subordinates. How is it the Delegating Style?
facts.truth: low task orientation and low relationship orientation
distractors: high task-orientation and low relationship orientation
high task-orientation and high relationship orientation
low task orientation and high relationship orientation

facts.context: Nonverbal and primarily nonculturally biased abilities are referred to as:
facts.truth: Fluid intelligence
distractors: Broad stratum abilities
Achievement or aptitude
Crystallized intelligence

facts.context: There are certain ways to increase external validity as well. From your knowledge about this ways, to what does the CLUSTER SAMPLING refer to?
facts.truth: sampling unit is a naturally occurring group
distractors: ensures the sample is representative of the population
ensures proportional representation
subjects and/or experimenter do no know purpose or which group they are in

facts.context: At the outset of group counseling, the therapist should explain char the obligation of the group member to respect confidentiality is
facts.truth: on the “honor system” only
distractors: less of an issue than it is in individual therapy
the legal responsibility of the therapist
protected by state/provincial laws

facts.context: Which of the following is true of strong organizational culture?
facts.truth: It is correlated with high levels of job performance
distractors: It can facilitate cultural and other types of change
It is correlated with low levels of job commitment
It is correlated with high levels of voluntary job turnover

facts.context: In a classroom setting, behaviors that are overt, lack a clear-cut beginning and end, and occur with moderate frequency, are best assessed by which of the following types of observational recording techniques
facts.truth: Interval
distractors: Event
Rating
Narrative

facts.context: When the spread of scores increases, the variance
facts.truth: increases
distractors: decreases
stays the same
can increase or decrease

facts.context: Attentional load theory posits:
facts.truth: The degree to which an ignored stimulus is processed depends on the extent of processing required by the attended stimulus
distractors: The unity of our actions places limits on attentional resources
Selective attention facilitates inhibition of return
There is a general limit to the extent of attentional resources

facts.context: Dr. Delbert Dinwoody is currently treating a 35-year-old client who has received a diagnosis of Alcohol Dependence. With the client's permission, Dr. Dinwoody invites the client's wife to participate in couple therapy. Inviting the wife to participate in therapy is:
facts.truth: acceptable as long as he believes couple therapy will benefit the client's progress in dealing with his alcohol problem.
distractors: acceptable as long as the wife is not receiving mental health services from another professional.
"unacceptable because in this situation, the wife is “vulnerable to undue inﬂuence."""
"unacceptable because doing so creates a ""conﬂict of interest.”"

facts.context: The most common cause of cerebral palsy is
facts.truth: trauma at the time of birth
distractors: dural hemorrhages
measles with accompanying fever
a genetic (autosomal) trait

facts.context: When the corpus callosum is cut in a right-handed patient, often the patient
facts.truth: cannot name objects placed in the left hand if the patient cannot see them
distractors: cannot carry out well-learned motor tasks with the left hand
Cannot name objects placed in the right hand if the patient can see them
shows a decline in verbal intelligence

facts.context: Amnesic Disorders describe conditions where there is memory impairment with no other significant cognitive impairments. Which of the following statements is incorrect in relation to Amnesic Disorders?
facts.truth: These disorders cause a loss of recall of learned information = anterograde amnesia.
distractors: Someone with condition confabulates to fill in gaps in the memory.
These disorders can be caused by any pathological process that affects brain areas associated with memory.
Amnesic disorders can be associated with hypnotics and sedatives

facts.context: An assessment that uses primarily observational methods to examine and understand the physical and psychological variables that impact behavior in a given environment or setting is a:
facts.truth: Ecological assessment
distractors: Functional behavioral assessment
Work sample
Norm-referenced test

facts.context: Asking an obese person to restrict eating to limited situations, such as a specific place in the kitchen at particular predetermined times of day, is best described as an example of
facts.truth: self-monitoring
distractors: stimulus control
response control
seif-punishment

facts.context: Babbling ordinarily begins at about 4 to 5 months of age and initially includes:
facts.truth: phonemes from all languages.
distractors: phonemes from the child’s native language only.
morphemes from all languages.
morphemes that the child has heard most frequently.

facts.context: For all sensory systems except olfaction, the afferent sensory pathways include the
facts.truth: thalamus
distractors: cerebellum
caudate nucleus
inferior colliculus

facts.context: A psychologist who is teaching a graduate course in counseling techniques at a state university has not updated the lecture notes or textbook selection for 7 years. This behavior is ethically questionable with respect to
facts.truth: competence
distractors: academic freedom
legal standards
humanitarian mission

facts.context: According to the Standards for Educational and Psychological Testing, when interpreting and reporting test results, one should
facts.truth: focus attention on the confidence interval of a score rather than on the obtained score itself
distractors: use descriptive labels, because such labels readily communicate specific meanings to most people
stress the obtained score, since it is the best estimate of the true score
exclude the potential effects of situational and demographic variables from consideration

facts.context: Form A of a standardized personality test was given in the fall and again in the spring co the same group of people. The reliability estimate that resulted from this research is referred to as
facts.truth: stability
distractors: external consistency
equivalence
internal consistency

facts.context: An employee of large manufacturing firm finds the work dull, and pursues the job only for the money. The employee performs minimally, and is not motivated so produce highest quantity or better quality. According to F. Herzberg's two-factor theory, the best way 10 increase this worker's self-motivation would be to
facts.truth: redesign the job to provide a challenge and a sense of accomplishment
distractors: offer a raise and incentive pay for greater productivity
increase opportunities for informed social interaction
provide the employee with a job tide carrying greater prestige

facts.context: Atkinson, Morten, and Sue’s (1993) Racial/Cultural Identity Development Model proposes that people in which of the following stages begin to question their rejection of all aspects of the dominant culture and absolute loyalty to their own culture?
facts.truth: introspection
distractors: encounter
dissonance
integrative awareness

facts.context: In the context of research design, external validity refers to the researcher’s ability to:
facts.truth: generalize the results of the study to other individuals, settings, and conditions.
distractors: manipulate the independent variable.
randomly assign participants to treatment groups.
determine whether there is a causal relationship between variables.
